stories.title: Paul Skenes' Best Start of 2026 — 8 Shutout Innings, 7 Ks vs. Arizona

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Paul Skenes last night vs. Arizona:

8 innings. 0 runs. 2 hits. 7 strikeouts.

The defending NL Cy Young winner is making his case again. Best start of 2026.

#MLB #PaulSkenes

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The NL Cy Young Award race just got interesting.

Paul Skenes threw 8 shutout innings against the Diamondbacks on Thursday. Two hits. Seven Ks.

If this is the baseline, good luck to everyone else.

#MLB #NLCyYoung

facebook.long_form:
```
The defending NL Cy Young Award winner is reminding everyone why he won it.

On Thursday night against the Arizona Diamondbacks, Paul Skenes threw eight shutout innings — two hits allowed, seven strikeouts — in what was described as arguably the best start of his 2026 season. The performance was dominant, efficient, and the kind of outing that makes hitters feel hopeless.

Skenes isn't just defending his award. He's making the argument that he should win it again.

The 2026 NL Cy Young conversation is already heating up early in May, and Skenes just inserted himself at the front of the line with Thursday's gem. It will be interesting to see how the national media reacts in the next few days — because this was a start that deserves recognition.

If this is what his ceiling looks like in 2026, the NL is in trouble.

What's your pick for NL Cy Young right now? Drop a comment below! ⬇️
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
8 innings. 0 runs. 7 strikeouts. Paul Skenes just had his best start of 2026 against the Diamondbacks. The reigning NL Cy Young is on another level. 🔥 #MLB #PaulSkenes
```

stories.title: Murakami's Historic Milestone — 14 HRs Before First Extra-Base Hit in MLB History

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Munetaka Murakami hit 14 home runs before recording his first career double or triple.

That has never happened in MLB history.

He's also tied with Aaron Judge for the league lead at 14 HRs. The White Sox rookie is different.

#WhiteSox #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
MLB home run leaders right now:

1. Munetaka Murakami — 14 HRs 🇯🇵
1. Aaron Judge — 14 HRs 🔥

Murakami had 14 HRs before his first double or triple. The history being made in Chicago is wild.

#WhiteSox #Yankees #MLB

facebook.long_form:
```
Here's a sentence that has never been written in MLB history:

A player hit 14 home runs before recording a single double or triple.

That's what Munetaka Murakami did before he finally hit his first career double on May 4.

The Chicago White Sox rookie — who signed a two-year, $34 million deal and made his MLB debut on March 26 — has been one of the most bizarre and captivating stories in baseball. He homers. That's essentially all he does for extra bases. He's tied for the MLB home run lead at 14 alongside Aaron Judge of the New York Yankees. At his current pace, projections say he could finish the season with over 50 home runs.

But the record that stopped everyone in their tracks is the one nobody expected: 14 career HRs before a double or triple. That's not just an MLB record — it's a fact that would sound made up if you said it out loud.

Murakami also became the first White Sox player ever, and just the fourth player in MLB history, to homer in each of his first three career games.

He's only been in the big leagues since late March. This is already a historic rookie season.

Are you watching Murakami this season? 👀
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
14 home runs before his first double or triple. An MLB record — never done before. Munetaka Murakami is having one of the most historic rookie seasons baseball has ever seen. 🔥 #WhiteSox #MLB
```

article.headline: Munetaka Murakami Sets MLB Record: 14 Home Runs Before His First Career Double or Triple

stories.title: Cubs 24-12, Leading NL Central — Hot Streak Rolls On

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
The Chicago Cubs are 24-12. They've won 18 of their last 21 games. They have 14 consecutive home wins — a franchise record.

The North Side has one of baseball's hottest teams right now.

#Cubs #NLCentral #MLB

facebook.long_form:
```
Is Chicago having a moment? Because this Cubs team is cooking.

Through 36 games, the Cubs are 24-12 — one of the best records in the National League and just a half game behind the Atlanta Braves for the best in the NL altogether. They've won 18 of their last 21 games. They hold a 3.5-game lead over St. Louis in the NL Central.

And at Wrigley Field? They've won 14 consecutive home games — a new franchise record.

It wasn't too long ago that the Cubs were an afterthought in the NL Central conversation. Now they're the team setting franchise records for home dominance and getting walk-off wins on back-to-back-to-back days (yes, that really happened earlier this week against Cincinnati).

The rotation has been reliable. The offense has been timely. And the home crowd at Wrigley has been electric all season.

If the Cubs keep this up, they aren't just a contender — they're the favorites in the NL Central, and maybe more.

Who saw this Cubs run coming? Let us know! ⬇️
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
24-12. 14 consecutive home wins (franchise record). 3.5 games up in the NL Central. The Chicago Cubs are one of baseball's best teams right now. 🐻 #Cubs #MLB #WrigleyField
```

stories.title: Jordan Walker Cardinals — .308, 10 HRs, .962 OPS Fueling St. Louis Surge

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Jordan Walker in 2025: .584 OPS
Jordan Walker in 2026: .962 OPS, .308 average, 10 home runs in 34 games

The Cardinals found their guy. And he's only 24 years old.

#Cardinals #MLB

facebook.long_form:
```
Remember when people weren't sure Jordan Walker would pan out?

In 2025, Walker posted a .584 OPS — below average, with questions about whether his tools would ever translate at the major league level. It was a frustrating year for the Cardinals and for a player who had been one of baseball's most hyped prospects.

Then 2026 arrived. And Jordan Walker arrived with it.

Through 34 games this season, Walker is hitting .308 with 10 home runs, 27 RBIs, and a .962 OPS. He's hitting the ball hard — with a max exit velocity of 97.1 mph — and he's been the engine behind a Cardinals offense that has already beaten the defending champion Dodgers in a series.

He just turned 24. He plays right field in St. Louis. And he is very much, very clearly, a star.

The Cardinals have built something interesting around Walker. They're 3.5 games back of the Cubs in the NL Central, but with this lineup clicking, the race is far from over.

Had Walker on your radar? Or did this season surprise you too? ⬇️
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
Jordan Walker: .308, 10 HR, .962 OPS, 34 games into 2026. The Cardinals' breakout star is making the NL Central very interesting. 🔥 #Cardinals #MLB #JordanWalker
```

article.headline: Jordan Walker's Breakout: .308, 10 Home Runs, .962 OPS Has the Cardinals Believing in 2026

stories.title: Travis Bazzana Gets First Career MLB Hit — Guardians' No. 1 Pick Arrives

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Travis Bazzana — No. 1 overall pick in the 2024 MLB Draft — just got his first career MLB hit with the Cleveland Guardians.

The journey from the top of the draft board to the big leagues is officially complete. 🙌

#Guardians #MLB

facebook.long_form:
```
Every great MLB career starts the same way — one hit at a time.

Travis Bazzana, the No. 1 overall pick in the 2024 MLB Draft, got his first career hit for the Cleveland Guardians in early May. The Australian second baseman — whom the Guardians selected with the very first pick in the entire draft — had a quiet debut (0-for-2 with two walks in a 1-0 loss to Tampa Bay), but the first hit arrived shortly after.

This is one of those moments that Guardians fans will remember for a long time. Bazzana was the consensus No. 1 pick in 2024 — the player Cleveland drafted to help rebuild the franchise and give their lineup an elite talent at the top. Every great career milestone matters, and the first hit is the one that makes everything feel real.

He's just getting started. But it's starting.

Guardians fans — what's your early take on Bazzana? ⬇️
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
Travis Bazzana — No. 1 overall pick in the 2024 MLB Draft — recorded his first career MLB hit for the Cleveland Guardians. The journey is just beginning. 🙌 #Guardians #MLB #TravisBazzana
```

stories.title: Braves Still MLB Best at 26-12 Despite Seattle Loss

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
The Atlanta Braves lost to Seattle 3-1 on Thursday.

Their record: 26-12. Still MLB's best.

They're scoring 5.61 runs per game — also the most in baseball. One loss doesn't change the story.

#Braves #MLB

facebook.long_form:
```
The Mariners beat the Braves 3-1 on Thursday.

And Atlanta is still the best team in baseball.

At 26-12, the Braves have MLB's top record. They're leading the majors with 5.61 runs per game scored — more than anyone. They play at home AND on the road with equal effectiveness. One loss to a good Mariners team doesn't change the 26-game story.

The Braves are deep, they can score, and they're built to win in October. One Thursday loss in early May is a data point, not a trend.

We'll check back in on Atlanta as the summer heats up, but right now? They're the team to beat.

Is Atlanta your early pick for the NL pennant? Drop it below. ⬇️
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
26-12. MLB's best record. 5.61 runs per game (most in baseball). One loss to Seattle doesn't change what the Braves are. 🪓 #Braves #MLB #AtlantaBraves
```
